questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a classroom atom model to check the key physics idea. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct?
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
The nucleus contains only electrons.
Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a revision diagram of a lithium atom to check the key physics idea. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct?
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
Protons are found in shells around the nucleus.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a student note about nuclei and shells to check the key physics idea. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct?
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
Neutrons move around the outside of the atom.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a particle model sketch to check the key physics idea. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct?
options: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
Electrons have the same positive charge as protons.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a classroom atom model to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
The nucleus contains no mass compared with the electrons.
Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a revision diagram of a lithium atom to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
Atoms contain no empty space between the nucleus and shells.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a student note about nuclei and shells to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
The electron shells contain protons and neutrons.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: A learner uses a particle model sketch to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct?
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.
Neutrons have a positive charge.
Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
All particles in an atom have the same charge.
questions.correctAnswer: Protons and neutrons are found in the nucleus.
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.
questions.senNote: The nucleus contains protons and neutrons. Electrons move around the nucleus.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a Geiger counter demonstration to check the key physics idea. What is background radiation?
options: Radiation produced only in nuclear power stations.
Radiation that appears only when a source is switched on.
Radiation that is present only in science laboratories.
The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
questions.correctAnswer: The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
questions.explanation: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.
questions.senNote: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a classroom radiation survey to check the key physics idea. What is background radiation?
options: Radiation produced only in nuclear power stations.
Radiation that appears only when a source is switched on.
The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
Radiation that means an object has become radioactive forever.
questions.correctAnswer: The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
questions.explanation: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.
questions.senNote: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a hospital safety briefing to check the key physics idea. What is background radiation?
options: Radiation produced only in nuclear power stations.
The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
Radiation made only by X-ray machines.
Radiation that is always too dangerous to measure.
questions.correctAnswer: The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
questions.explanation: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.
questions.senNote: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a background count-rate log to check the key physics idea. What is background radiation?
options: The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
Radiation produced only in nuclear power stations.
Radiation made only by X-ray machines.
Radiation that comes only from human-made sources.
questions.correctAnswer: The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
questions.explanation: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.
questions.senNote: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.

options: The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
Radiation that exists only inside medical equipment.

Radiation made only by X-ray machines.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a nuclear power station diagram to check the key physics idea. Which statement about nuclear fission is correct?
options: Electrons split out of atoms and cause chemical burning.
A neutral atom turns into a charged atom only because it gains heat.
The outer electron shells split and release gravitational energy.
A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.correctAnswer: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.explanation: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.
questions.senNote: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a star-energy explanation to check the key physics idea. Which statement about nuclear fission is correct?
options: Electrons split out of atoms and cause chemical burning.
A neutral atom turns into a charged atom only because it gains heat.
A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
A large nucleus cools down and becomes chemically neutral.
questions.correctAnswer: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.explanation: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.
questions.senNote: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a reactor fuel description to check the key physics idea. Which statement about nuclear fission is correct?
options: Electrons split out of atoms and cause chemical burning.
A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
A neutral atom turns into a charged atom only because it gains heat.
Two atoms rub together and release sound energy.
questions.correctAnswer: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.explanation: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.
questions.senNote: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a fusion research note to check the key physics idea. Which statement about nuclear fission is correct?
options: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
Electrons split out of atoms and cause chemical burning.
A neutral atom turns into a charged atom only because it gains heat.
The nucleus loses all of its protons but stays the same element.
questions.correctAnswer: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.explanation: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.
questions.senNote: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a nuclear power station diagram to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about nuclear fission is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: Electrons split out of atoms and cause chemical burning.
A neutral atom turns into a charged atom only because it gains heat.
A magnetic field splits the electrons into smaller particles.
A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.correctAnswer: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.explanation: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.
questions.senNote: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a star-energy explanation to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about nuclear fission is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: Electrons split out of atoms and cause chemical burning.
Two large nuclei join together without changing mass.
A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
A nucleus absorbs light and turns into an electron.
questions.correctAnswer: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.explanation: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.
questions.senNote: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a radiographer safety poster to check the key physics idea. Which statement correctly compares contamination and irradiation?
options: Contamination and irradiation both stop as soon as a lead sheet is used.
Both words mean the same thing in radiation safety.
Irradiation always leaves radioactive atoms inside the object.
Contamination means unwanted radioactive material is on or in an object, but irradiation means exposure to radiation only.
questions.correctAnswer: Contamination means unwanted radioactive material is on or in an object, but irradiation means exposure to radiation only.
questions.explanation: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.
questions.senNote: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a sealed-source demonstration to check the key physics idea. Which statement correctly compares contamination and irradiation?
options: Contamination and irradiation both stop as soon as a lead sheet is used.
Both words mean the same thing in radiation safety.
Contamination means unwanted radioactive material is on or in an object, but irradiation means exposure to radiation only.
Contamination is exposure to radiation only, with no material transfer.
questions.correctAnswer: Contamination means unwanted radioactive material is on or in an object, but irradiation means exposure to radiation only.
questions.explanation: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.
questions.senNote: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.

Both words mean the same thing in radiation safety.
Irradiation can be washed off the skin like dust.
questions.correctAnswer: Contamination means unwanted radioactive material is on or in an object, but irradiation means exposure to radiation only.
questions.explanation: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.
questions.senNote: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.

questions.explanation: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.
